Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are provided an event log from a P2P process with columns Case ID Timestamp Activity The instructions say We have a table that shows events for each case Our task is to analyze the sequence of activities and identify any anomalies or unusual sequences relative to typical Procure to Pay business processes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This sequence appears typical in P2P processes Typically process might go requisition approval purchase order issuance receipt of goods vendor invoice submission or invoice received and then matching invoice to purchase order payment release

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 appears normal check common P2P sequences In case 1001 Vendor Invoice Received occurs after Receive Goods and then Match Invoice to PO and Release Payment That seems correct

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 the order in case 1002 is not in chronological sequence We have Create PR then Issue PO But there is no explicit approval event before issuing purchase order Instead that event appears later in the timeline After issue PO next event is vendor invoice received Then release payment occurs Then later Receive Goods is recorded And then Match Invoice to PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The typical process should be requisition is approved first then PO issued Additionally receiving goods occurs after vendor invoice is already received and even after release of payment That is unusual because normally vendor should not be paid until the receipt confirmation or matching invoice Payment release is done only after invoice is matched to PO and goods are confirmed as received

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is problematic because they seem to have bypassed approval and then released payment prematurely This may suggest a lack of proper controls and potential for fraud because you are paying before confirming that the goods have been delivered

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Payment is released before receiving vendor invoice and goods receipt Order is Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Issue Purchase Order then Release Payment then Vendor Invoice Received then Receive Goods then Match Invoice to PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Typically the process should have vendor invoice arrival and receipt of goods before matching with PO and then releasing payment In this case payment was released well before vendor invoice is received and before receiving goods

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This might be problematic because it indicates that payment was processed without proper documentation and without confirming delivery If invoice arrives after payment then there is no proper audit trail Payment release based on incomplete information can lead to disputes or reconciliation issues

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Receive Goods occurs before Issue Purchase Order In typical P2P you create requisition then get approval then issue a purchase order which triggers delivery and then receive goods But here it seems reversed They received goods first then issued the purchase order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The purchase order should be issued before goods receipt usually Receiving goods without having an official purchase order can indicate unauthorized procurement because there is no formal authorization in place when goods arrive

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: It might mean that the goods were delivered informally and then a PO was retroactively created That could be risky from a compliance perspective and may indicate miscommunication or fraud

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Summary of anomalies Case 1001 appears normal Case 1002 shows multiple anomalies including approval out of sequence and payment before goods receipt Case 1003 shows payment before invoice and goods receipt Case 1004 shows goods receipt before purchase order issuance
